A.reason: Delivering words that touch another someone, plus telling recipient they must act or bad consequence follows — captures the legal-summons force.

B.missing: no WORDS/writ, no legal obligation; just moving an object near someone
B.reason: Generic delivery of a thing near someone, could be any handing-over; no writ/summons content.

C.reason: A kind of words done/placed at another someone plus saying it to them — captures delivery of written summons upon a person.

D.missing: no delivery/placement upon recipient; only doing with, seeing, knowing words
D.reason: Vaguely about words and being seen but does not render delivering a writ upon someone.

E.missing: no WORDS/writ, no legal summons content
E.reason: Someone does a thing and it is at another someone — fits countless delivery scenarios.

F.missing: no WORDS/writ; touching an object to someone is generic
F.reason: Generic delivery-touch of an unspecified thing, no summons or written document.

G.missing: thing touches recipient but no WORDS/writ identity
G.reason: Adds saying to addressee but the delivered thing is unspecified, not identified as a writ/summons.
